name: JAWS

usage_sections: Files and Folders with Full Control or Modify Access

arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with winsiderss/systeminformer (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with ohpe/juicy-potato (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with libyal/libevt (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: JAWS - Just Another Windows (Enum) Script.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: JAWS - Just Another Windows (Enum) Script.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `CMD C:\temp> powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\jaws-enum.ps1 -OutputFilename JAWS-Enum.txt`. Further invocations from upstream: `CMD C:\temp> powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\jaws-enum.ps1`; `PS C:\temp> .\jaws-enum.ps1 -OutputFileName Jaws-Enum.txt`. JAWS - Just Another Windows (Enum) Script.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Penetration Testing, use JAWS because JAWS - Just Another Windows (Enum) Script.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `CMD C:\temp> powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\jaws-enum.ps1 -OutputFilename JAWS-Enum.txt` as documented upstream for JAWS.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `CMD C:\temp> powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\jaws-enum.ps1` as documented upstream for JAWS.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `PS C:\temp> .\jaws-enum.ps1 -OutputFileName Jaws-Enum.txt` as documented upstream for JAWS.
When chaining Penetration Testing tools, pair JAWS with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `JAWS --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
